summary: Backend-only testing for Performance Fitness API. 19/19 pytest cases passed (100%). Covered: root health, auth bearer flows (valid/invalid/missing), profile PUT/GET with Mifflin-St Jeor targets, meals analyze via Claude Sonnet 4.5 vision, meals list (no image_base64 leak) + delete, activity upsert/get, workouts generate requiring profile + 400 without profile + week/today/complete, transformations create (Claude feedback) + list, dashboard day (target/consumed/macros/burned breakdown/activity/balance) + week (7-day rolling + avg_consumed), logout invalidates session. Verified no MongoDB _id leaks in any response. Real JPEG meal & person photos used for vision endpoints.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown'); migrate to lifespan context manager (non-blocking).
CORS middleware is added AFTER app.include_router; works in practice but conventionally should be added before include_router for clarity.

compute_targets stores BMR as rounded int; for very low intake the floor of 1200 kcal guard is good. Note: protein 2g/kg can exceed calorie budget for low-weight 'lose' goals (carbs would clamp at 0); acceptable for MVP.
image_base64 stored in meals (capped at 200k chars) / transformations (300k) inside MongoDB documents — fine for MVP but consider object storage later to keep doc size below 16MB.
seed_data_creation: Seeded user_test_001 + user_sessions(test_session_token_abc, 7d TTL) in MongoDB via conftest session fixture. Auto-cleans users/sessions/profiles/meals/activity/workouts/transformations for user_test_001 after suite.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_fitness_api.py for regression. Real JPEG images are downloaded to /tmp/meal.jpg and /tmp/person.jpg before tests; if missing, fetch from Unsplash (URLs in chat). Public base URL comes from frontend/.env EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL.
rca_of_issue: No bugs found; all 19 endpoints behaved as specified. Auth bearer flow, vision endpoints (Claude Sonnet 4.5 via emergentintegrations), persistence, and dashboard aggregation all returned expected shapes with no _id leakage.
